for the assessing of all such as have not contributed upon the propositions of both Houses of Parliament for raising of money plate, horse, horsemen, and armes, for defence of the King, kingdom, and Parliament, or have not contributed proportionably according to their estates : with an explanation of the former ordinance, declaring that if any person so assessed, shall within 6 or 12 daies, after notice thereof, pay in the money to the treasurers in Guildhall, they shall give acquittances for the same, as usuall, to be repaied upon the publique faith : but if they refuse to pay, the collectors shal have power to cistrein their goods, imprison their persons and families of such disafected persons, shal no longer remain within the city of London : likewise an ordinance of both Houses of Parliament for the better provision of victuals and other necessaries for the army, and for payment and satisfaction to be made for the same.

for new loans and contributions as well from the united provinces of Holland as from England and Wales for the speedy relief of the miserable and distressed estate of the Protestants in the kingdom of Ireland : whose lives are dayly sacrificed not only to the malice of their and our bloudy enemies, the popish rebels, but likewise to starving, cold, and hunger.

first_sentence: Baron Pompeo was an interesting man.     My Dad's uncle.  Pompeo's brother was Alfred Louis Aloisi.   Baron Pompeo Aloisi was second in command to Mussolini. Basically his "right arm" and  a Chief Delegate in the League of Nations.  It's funny because His brother Alfred Aloisi (my grandfather) started the first American Legion in Paris, France.  He was assigned to duties as part of Stratedic Services  (OSSD).  The brother were enemies.  Pompeo wanted war  and Alfred wanted the end of hostilities.
text: Speech delivered by Baron Aloisi, head of the Italian delegation, to the League of Nations meeting of the Assembly, October 9th, 1935
Speech delivered by Baron Aloisi, head of the Italian delegation, to the League of Nations meeting of the Assembly, October 9th, 1935.

Baron Pompeo was an interesting man.     My Dad's uncle.  Pompeo's brother was Alfred Louis Aloisi.   Baron Pompeo Aloisi was second in command to Mussolini. Basically his "right arm" and  a Chief Delegate in the League of Nations.  It's funny because His brother Alfred Aloisi (my grandfather) started the first American Legion in Paris, France.  He was assigned to duties as part of Stratedic Services  (OSSD).  The brother were enemies.  Pompeo wanted war  and Alfred wanted the end of hostilities.
